name: Zimbabwe Cyber and Data Protection Act Self-Assessment
description: Configurable maturity-based self-assessment aligned to the Zimbabwe Cyber and Data Protection Act.

sections.title: Governance and accountability
sections.summary: Leadership ownership, policies, roles, and oversight for data protection.

controls.title: Privacy accountability structure
controls.findingTemplate: Formal privacy accountability roles are not clearly defined.
controls.recommendedAction: Appoint a responsible privacy lead and approve governance responsibilities.

questions.prompt: How clearly are privacy responsibilities assigned and documented?
questions.helpText: Consider board oversight, management ownership, and operational roles.

sections.title: Personal data inventory and classification
sections.summary: Visibility of personal data, processing activities, and sensitive classifications.

controls.title: Personal data inventory
controls.findingTemplate: The organisation does not maintain a reliable inventory of personal data processing.
controls.recommendedAction: Create and maintain a data inventory covering systems, categories, purposes, and retention.

questions.prompt: How complete and current is your inventory of personal data and sensitive data?
questions.helpText: Include systems, owners, locations, and cross-border transfers where relevant.

sections.title: Lawful processing and consent
sections.summary: Legal basis, consent controls, and purpose limitation practices.

controls.title: Lawful basis and consent evidence
controls.findingTemplate: Lawful bases or consent records are incomplete for one or more processing activities.
controls.recommendedAction: Document lawful bases per process and implement evidence-backed consent records where required.

questions.prompt: How consistently are lawful bases and consent records captured for processing activities?
questions.helpText: Assess documented rationale, approval, and traceability.

sections.title: Transparency and notices
sections.summary: Privacy notices, communication clarity, and fair processing disclosure.

controls.title: Privacy notice management
controls.findingTemplate: Privacy notices are missing, outdated, or not aligned to actual processing.
controls.recommendedAction: Review and publish privacy notices for each major processing context.

questions.prompt: How well do your privacy notices describe data use, rights, and contact points?
questions.helpText: Review websites, forms, HR notices, and customer onboarding materials.

sections.title: Data subject rights
sections.summary: Processes for access, correction, objection, deletion, and complaint handling.

controls.title: Rights request handling
controls.findingTemplate: Data subject requests are not centrally tracked or managed to defined timelines.
controls.recommendedAction: Establish a request workflow, register, and ownership model for rights handling.

questions.prompt: How consistently can your organisation receive, log, and respond to rights requests?
questions.helpText: Consider intake channels, identity checks, tracking, and escalation.

sections.title: Security and breach response
sections.summary: Security safeguards, incident handling, and breach notification readiness.

controls.title: Security safeguards
controls.findingTemplate: Security controls are not consistently implemented or reviewed for personal data processing.
controls.recommendedAction: Define baseline safeguards, assign owners, and monitor control operation.

questions.prompt: How mature are your security safeguards for protecting personal data?
questions.helpText: Include access control, backups, logging, awareness, and technical hardening.

controls.title: Breach response readiness
controls.findingTemplate: Breach response procedures are not fully documented, tested, or aligned to notification requirements.
controls.recommendedAction: Create a breach response playbook and test notification decision-making.

questions.prompt: How prepared is your organisation to detect, assess, escalate, and report a personal data breach?
questions.helpText: Consider decision trees, contacts, evidence handling, and rehearsal.

sections.title: Third parties and cross-border transfers
sections.summary: Processor governance, contractual controls, and international transfer oversight.

controls.title: Third-party assurance and transfer governance
controls.findingTemplate: Third-party processing and cross-border transfer arrangements are not sufficiently governed.
controls.recommendedAction: Assess processors, maintain contracts, and document transfer safeguards.

questions.prompt: How robust are your controls over service providers and cross-border transfers of personal data?
questions.helpText: Assess due diligence, contracts, shared responsibilities, and transfer approvals.
